extra.message: SQL injection vulnerability: Username parameter from query string is directly interpolated into SQL query using f-string without sanitization or parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL commands by providing malicious input like: admin" OR "1"="1

SQL injection vulnerability: All user-supplied fields (name, username, password, address, email, contact) from the User request body are directly interpolated into INSERT SQL query via f-string without parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL commands through any of these fields.

SQL injection vulnerability: Username parameter is directly interpolated into DELETE SQL query using f-string without sanitization. An attacker can bypass the DELETE clause and execute arbitrary SQL commands.

SQL injection vulnerability: User address field is directly interpolated into DELETE SQL query without even surrounding quotes. This makes SQL injection trivial to exploit. An attacker can inject expressions like: 1; DROP TABLE users; --

NoSQL injection vulnerability: The raw JSON request body is passed directly as a MontyDB query without validation or sanitization. An attacker can inject MongoDB operators like {"username": {"$ne": ""}} to dump all users, or use $where/$regex operators for data exfiltration.
